_README: Baseline allowlist for scripts/check-tenant-isolation.ts. Each entry is a route file (path relative to apps/unified-portal) that the heuristic currently flags: it queries a tenant-scoped table via a service-role client but contains no recognizable scoping token (tenant_id / developer_user_id / assertCanAccessDevelopment / requireRole(['super_admin']) / verified-ownership marker '// tenant-scope:'). The checker exits non-zero only when a NEW file is flagged that is not listed here, so today the build passes while regressions are caught. IMPORTANT: many entries below are CONFIRMED CROSS-TENANT HOLES from the 2026-06 audit, not safe cases — they are seeded here only to establish a passing baseline and should be DRAINED (fixed + removed from this list), not treated as approved. 'classification' records the audit verdict; drain the HOLE/SUSPICIOUS entries first.

allow.reason: Authenticated (requireRole) but units.select('*') has no tenant_id filter — returns every tenant's homeowners (name/email/address).
Agent-profile scoped via getUser + agent_profiles.user_id; reads developments by client activeDevelopmentId without an ownership check (minor cross-tenant development-name read).

False positive: RLS-bound session client (createServerSupabaseClient), getUser auth, intelligence_actions filtered by developer_id = user.id; developments read derived from the user's own actions (ownership chain).
Mutates pending_drafts by client draftId resolved from recent_actions(batchId) with no workspace/tenant check (unlike sibling draft routes that use authorizeDraftMutation).
getAdminSession auth but units query has no tenant_id filter; returns platform-wide homeowner counts + per-project breakdown; client developmentId unverified.
Pre-auth login flow; resolves a unit by login code to issue a session. No session exists yet by design.
requireRole auth but document_sections query has no tenant/project scope; client projectId unverified — cross-tenant document metadata.
requireRole auth but messages/documents queried by client [id] (developmentId) with no ownership check; units count uses a hardcoded project id.
No auth at all. GET reads and PUT WRITES developments.prehandover_config by client [id] — unauthenticated cross-tenant read+write.
No auth. Generates a QR/PDF pack of units (incl. purchaser_name) for a hardcoded project id — unauthenticated PII export.
No auth. Returns all units (purchaser_name, addresses, milestone dates) for client [id] — fully public cross-tenant PII read.
Liveness probe; counts document_sections only. No tenant data of substance, excluded from middleware matcher by design.
Accepted token pattern: the unit_uid handover code IS the scope. (Minor: returns purchaser_name on a valid code; codes should retain entropy.)
Registration claim flow keyed on the unit id from the prior lookup-code step; first-claim-wins, rejects already-claimed units.
getAdminSession auth but unit fetched by id with NO tenant check (code self-documents the skip); returns full homeowner PII + message history cross-tenant.
requireRole auth; reads session.tenantId then ignores it — document_sections queried by client developmentId with no ownership check.
No auth. Counts documents/sections/messages platform-wide (aggregate disclosure).
No auth. Returns project name/address + unit counts for any client projectId.
Authenticated via getAdminContextFromSession; creates a project/units under a client-supplied requestedOrgId — verify org ownership is enforced.
requireRole auth but lists ALL projects and counts units (project_id only, no tenant filter) — cross-tenant catalog + unit counts.
requireRole auth but UPDATES units and unit_sales_pipeline by client unitId with no tenant_id/ownership check — cross-tenant write.
No auth. With no/blank developmentId returns EVERY unit's purchaser_name + address across all tenants; client developmentId unverified.
requireRole(['super_admin','admin']) — the 'admin' (tenant-scoped) role can PUT developments.system_instructions for any development by client development_id (cross-tenant AI-prompt write).
requireRole(['super_admin','admin']) reads question_analytics/units/developments platform-wide with no tenant filter; cross-tenant for the 'admin' role (intended super-only).
No auth in handler; /api/super/* is NOT protected by middleware. Returns unit counts for any/all tenants.
No auth and no token validation; reads units + documents (incl. file_url) by bare unit id — cross-tenant document exposure once a unit id is known.
False positive: OAuth/magic-link callback uses the user's RLS-bound session client (createRouteHandlerClient + exchangeCodeForSession), not a service-role client; units read/update governed by RLS.
